data.title: Blackstone Q1 earnings: Real estate strength, private credit in spotlight
data.description:
When Blackstone (BX) announces first-quarter earnings on Thursday, Wall Street expects the private equity giant to post an EPS of $1.34, implying a 22.9% increase, while revenue is expected to rise 4.3% to $3.40B for the quarter.

Last quarter, the alternative asset manager turned in better-than-expected Q4 earnings on the back of increased inflows, better-than-expected fee-related earnings, and continued growth in management and advisory fees.

During its Q4 earnings call, Blackstone CFO Michael Chae said that the firm expects management fees to continue on a strong positive trajectory in 2026, underpinned by robust growth in private equity credit, insurance, and multi-asset investing segments, with real estate management fees remaining consistent with Q4 levels in the near term.

However, the alternative asset manager’s shares are down nearly 15% year-to-date, underperforming the benchmark S&P 500, which has gained 3.2% in the same period.

Earlier this month, Oppenheimer upgraded private equity giant Blackstone (BX) to Outperform from Perform as the stock has recently taken a "drubbing," despite its "outstanding performance," especially in its real estate arm.

Analyst Chris Kotowski said that a distinctive feature of BX is its real estate business, which until recently was its largest generator of fees.

“This real estate business, in our view, is without peer (with Brookfield a somewhat distant #2) and represents significant upside in the coming years,” Kotowski added.

Seeking Alpha analysts and Wall Street analysts are bullish and consider it a buying opportunity as the stock is down despite record earnings last quarter and resilient fundamentals.

Seeking Alpha analyst Pacifica Yield rated the stock a Buy, saying that redemption pressures in BX's private credit funds are being driven by sentiment, not an actual spike in non-performing loans or defaults.

“Despite market fears around private credit defaults and redemptions, BX's AUM and fee-earning AUM continue to grow, with fee-earning AUM growing by 11% year-over-year to $921.7B,” the analyst highlighted.

Over the last two years, BX has beaten EPS estimates 88% of the time and revenue estimates 50% of the time.

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have seen no upward revisions and 11 downward revisions, while revenue estimates have seen no upward revisions against two downward moves.

data.title: Marijuana MSOs surge on report Trump set to formally reclassify cannabis
data.description:
• President Trump will reschedule marijuana as a Schedule III drug under the Controlled Substances Act (CSA), a major move that would allow for greater research of the plant, make it easier for pharma companies to develop cannabis-based medicines, and reduce some legal penalties associated with marijuana possession. 
  • Axios, citing an administration official, said the reclassification could come as soon as Wednesday.
  • Multi-state operators are surging on the report. 
  • Trump's decision, however, would fall short of legalizing marijuana on the federal level. Its manufacture, distribution, and possession would still be considered illegal under the CSA.
  • In December, Trump said he was "very strongly" looking at an Executive Order to reclassify marijuana. 
  • Multi-state operators: Ascend Wellness (OTCQX:AAWH), Curaleaf Holdings (OTCPK:CURLF), Cresco Labs (OTCQX:CRLBF), Green Thumb Industries (OTCQX:GTBIF), Trulieve Cannabis (OTCQX:TCNNF), Ayr Wellness (OTCQX:AYRWF), Verano Holdings (VRNO), and Jushi Holdings (OTCQX:JUSHF).
  • Cannabis ETFs: AdvisorShares Pure Cannabis ETF (NYSEARCA:YOLO), Amplify Alternative Harvest ETF (MJUS), and Amplify Seymour Cannabis ETF (NYSEARCA:CNBS).

data.title: Convenience store chain Yesway rallies in opening IPO trading
data.description:
Yesway (YSWY) opened for trading at $22 per share after pricing its IPO at $20 per share, which was the low end of the original range. The stock was swapping hands as high as $22.75 before settling back a bit. The market cap on Yesway (YSWAY) is close to $1.4B after the company raised $280M with the offering.

Yesway (YSWAY) calls itself one of the fastest-growing convenience store operators in the US, with 419 company-operated stores as of December 31, with 90 successful openings of new stores from 2020 to 2025. The geographic footprint consists of stores located in attractive rural and suburban markets across the Southwest and Midwest under the Yesway and Allsup's brand names. The greatest concentration of stores is in Texas, which has recorded the largest absolute population increase in the U.S. since 2020.

The company generated revenue of $2.67B in calendar year 2025 (+5% Y/Y), adjusted EBITDA of $187M, and net income of $54M.

Yesway (YSWY) was founded in 2015 and backed by Brookwood Financial Partners. It grew rapidly through both new-store development and acquisitions, most notably the 2019 purchase of Allsup’s, which strengthened its presence in the Southwest and expanded its foodservice business. Yesway (YSWY) operates more than 444 locations across nine states and continues to focus on store expansion, remodels, fuel upgrades, and operational improvements.

Major competitors include 7-Eleven, Casey’s General Stores (CASY), Murphy USA (MUSA), QuikTrip, and Alimentation Couche-Tard's (ATD:CA) (ANCTF) (ALCTY) Circle K.

data.title: Intel Q1 Earnings Preview: Server CPUs seen as key driver, but foundry, margin pressures remain
data.description:
Intel (INTC) is scheduled to announce Q1 earnings results on Thursday, April 23rd, after market close.

Ahead of the earnings, HSBC issued an upgrade on Tuesday as the investment firm called the continued demand for server CPUs a “bigger catalyst” for the semiconductor giant than its foundry push.

Analyst Frank Lee expects upside to Intel's server CPU business to start in the second-quarter, as he expects the company's revenue to be $14.2B, 9% above the Wall Street estimate. Further boosting Intel's results could be price hikes that aid its gross margins, Lee added.

In the same week, BNP Paribas upgraded the semiconductor firm ahead of its Q1 earnings, with analyst David O'Connor saying that Intel is likely to benefit from increased server CPU demand caused by agentic artificial intelligence.

Stifel, meanwhile, said in an earnings preview that Intel (INTC) enters with an improving narrative, server CPU demand well above expectations, and the September Nvidia (NVDA) partnership validating the x86 ecosystem, but gross margins are likely to remain static in low-to-mid 30s% in the near term and foundry losses remain a headwind.

More recent SA contributor analyses have been bearish of the stock, with Doug Collins noting that recent high-profile deals with Google (GOOG) (GOOGL), Amazon (AMZN) and the Terafab project (TSLA) validate Intel’s technology but lack near-term revenue impact or clear financial details, and the foundry segment remains “deeply unprofitable.”

The Value Portfolio also sees Intel facing intense competition from TSMC, Samsung, and new entrants, and despite progress with 18A/14A nodes and partnerships like Nvidia, the foundry division posts ~$10B in annualized losses, and revenue/margins continue to decline Y/Y.

The consensus EPS estimate is $0.01 (-92.3% Y/Y) and the consensus revenue estimate is $12.43B (-2.1% Y/Y).

Over the last 2 years, INTC has beaten EPS estimates 63% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 75% of the time.

Over the last 3 months, EPS estimates have seen 8 upward revisions and 22 downward. Revenue estimates have seen 11 upward revisions and 20 downward.

data.title: Exxon weighing sale of Hong Kong gas stations for ~$500M - Bloomberg
data.description:
Exxon Mobil (XOM) is exploring a sale of its gasoline station network in Hong Kong in a deal that could fetch $500M-$600M for the assets, Bloomberg reported Wednesday, as the company continues to trim its retail footprint globally.

The potential sale of the 39 Esso-branded service stations comes at a time of uncertainty for the retail fuel industry, as the business faces renewed pressure from extreme volatility in crude markets following the outbreak of the Middle East war.

Hong Kong gasoline prices are among the highest in the world, averaging more than $15/gal, and the local government has been encouraging a switch to electric vehicles.

Exxon (XOM) has a global strategy to streamline assets and has appointed advisors to handle sales of its downstream retail sites under the Esso brand in markets including France, New Zealand, and Hong Kong, according to the report; in October, the company agreed to sell its Esso-branded service stations in Singapore to PT Chandra Asri Pacific.

data.title: Lutnick says Nvidia's H200 AI chips not yet sold in China
data.description:
Nvidia's (NVDA) H200 AI chips have not yet ​been sold to Chinese companies, according to Commerce Department Secretary Howard ‌Lutnick, due to difficulties faced by those companies in getting permission from China's government, Reuters reported.

"The Chinese central government has not ​let them, as of yet, buy the chips, because ​they're trying to keep their investment focused on ⁠their own domestic industry," said Lutnick when asked about H200 ​sales to China, the report added.

"We have not sold them chips as of ​yet,"  Lutnick added.

Nvidia did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

Last month, it was reported that Nvidia (NVDA) won China's ​approval to sell its second-most powerful AI chip, the H200, in the Asian country. The H200 is the predecessor to Nvidia's current flagship Blackwell chips and the upcoming Vera Rubin. In December 2025, U.S. President Donald Trump approved the sale of some advanced AI chips, including the H200 GPUs, to China in exchange for a 25% fee. Trump issued a proclamation in January imposing the 25% tariff on certain high-end chips, including Nvidia's H200 and AMD's (AMD) MI325X.

Nvidia's CEO Jensen Huang said last month that the company had received purchase orders from customers in China. 

Shipments of ‌the ⁠chips have been hindered by disagreements over the terms of the sales in China and the U.S., the report noted.

On Wednesday, Lutnick was also asked if the Trump ​administration planned to ​reimpose a ⁠rule that would have curbed shipments of advanced U.S. technology to thousands of Chinese companies. ​The measure, known as the affiliates rule, was ​delayed ⁠for a year in November as part of a trade negotiation with China.

"I agree that the affiliates rule is a smart ⁠thing ​for the United States of America ​to consider, but it is part of the balance of that full trade ​agreement," said Lutnick.

data.title: Starbucks' $100M Nashville investment fuels speculation over Seattle exit
data.description:
As part of the company’s efforts to expand coffeehouses across the Southeast, Starbucks (SBUX) will invest $100M to open a corporate office in Nashville, Tennessee, to employ as many as 2K workers over the next five years. Besides state tax breaks, Starbucks (SBUX) will receive a year’s free rent at the new location.

Certain operations currently being conducted at the Seattle headquarters will be moved to Nashville once the facility is completed in 2027. These include roles tied to the Southeast expansion, functions previously outsourced to contractors, and certain unspecified roles currently performed by Seattle-based employees. Some technology positions in Seattle will also be transferred to Nashville, most likely to avoid a 6.5% Washington state tax on IT services.

While most of the company’s corporate staff will remain in Seattle, Washington’s tax structure and business climate have led to speculation that Niccol’s move to Nashville may signal a gradual shift away from Seattle. Last November, Seattle’s then mayor-elect Katie Wilson urged a boycott of the company during a strike of the company’s baristas.

While at Chipotle (CMG), Niccol moved the company’s headquarters to California from Denver, Colorado.

Separately, beginning May 11, Starbucks (SBUX) customers can order coffee up to one hour ahead of time using the Mobile Order & Pay app.

Scheduled ordering allows customers to select a pickup time when placing their order instead of ordering for immediate pickup.

Powered by Starbucks’ (SBUX) Smart Queue algorithm, pickup times reflect real-time availability at each location.

data.title: NVR stock slides after Q1 earnings disappoint
data.description:
NVR (NVR) stock dropped 5.9% in Wednesday midday trading after the homebuilder and mortgage banking company's Q1 earnings failed to meet the Wall Street consensus amid a weak housing market.

Q1 EPS of $67.76,  missing the average analyst estimate of $79.20, declined from $94.83 in the year-ago period.

Q1 revenue of $1.88B, vs. the $2.02B consensus, decreased from $2.41B a year ago.

Settlements in Q1 sank 22% Y/Y to 4,015, primarily from a 15% lower backlog balance of homes sold but not settled entering Q1 2026 compared to the same period in 2025. The average settlement price of $457K stayed flat from the year-ago quarter.

New orders rose 7% to 5,738 units, while the average sales price of new orders slipped 2% to $440.1K.

The cancellation rate improved to 14% from 16% in Q1 2025.

Homebuilding revenue of $1.83B fell 22%. Gross profit margin narrowed to 19.6% from 21.9% a year ago on pricing pressure and higher lot costs. 

Mortgage banking loan production totaled $1.05B, down 27% Y/Y. 

NVR's (NVR) effective tax rate in Q1 declined to 21.2% from 25.5% in the same period last year.
